# UC-22.1.26 · GDPR Resilience and Availability Monitoring for Personal-Data Services

## Description

Integrity, confidentiality, and availability of processing systems are core Art. 32 objectives. This use case monitors uptime, error rates, and recovery objectives for applications and databases documented in the ROPA as processing personal data — evidencing resilience measures such as clustering, failover drills, and synthetic checks.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for GDPR compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes itsi_summary, synthetic. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Map ROPA `system_name` to ITSI services in `itsi_service_map.csv`; (2) define KPI thresholds aligned to internal RPO/RTO for personal-data services; (3) alert when health scores breach SLO; (4) attach failover drill references from `gdpr_security_test_register.csv`; (5) report monthly resilience posture to the DPO. Indexes required: `index=itsi_summary`, `index=synthetic`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk ITSI (Splunkbase 1841), Splunk Synthetic Monitoring.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=itsi_summary` (service health), `index=synthetic` or HEC synthetic results, `gdpr_ropa_register.csv`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Map ROPA `system_name` to ITSI services in `itsi_service_map.csv`; (2) define KPI thresholds aligned to internal RPO/RTO for personal-data services; (3) alert when health scores breach SLO; (4) attach failover drill references from `gdpr_security_test_register.csv`; (5) report monthly resilience posture to the DPO.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
| inputlookup gdpr_ropa_register.csv WHERE data_category!="N/A"
| fields system_name
| lookup itsi_service_map.csv system_name OUTPUT itsi_service
| join itsi_service [
    search index=itsi_summary earliest=-24h
    | stats latest(health_score) as health_score latest(severity) as severity by itsi_service
]
| eval breach_slo=if(health_score < 70 OR match(lower(severity),"(?i)critical|high"), 1, 0)
| where breach_slo=1
| table system_name, itsi_service, health_score, severity
```

Understanding this SPL

**GDPR Resilience and Availability Monitoring for Personal-Data Services (Art. 32(1)(b)(c))** — Integrity, confidentiality, and availability of processing systems are core Art. 32 objectives. This use case monitors uptime, error rates, and recovery objectives for applications and databases documented in the ROPA as processing personal data — evidencing resilience measures such as clustering, failover drills, and synthetic checks.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=itsi_summary` (service health), `index=synthetic` or HEC synthetic results, `gdpr_ropa_register.csv`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk ITSI (Splunkbase 1841), Splunk Synthetic Monitoring. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Loads rows via `inputlookup` (KV store or CSV lookup) for enrichment or reporting.
• Keeps or drops fields with `fields` to shape columns and size.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **breach_slo** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where breach_slo=1` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: breach_slo, health_score, itsi_service, severity, system_name.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with service health in the ITSI views and the operational runbook for the same service window.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Single value (services breaching SLO), Table (affected personal-data services), Line chart (health score trend), Heatmap (severity by hour).

## SPL

```spl
| inputlookup gdpr_ropa_register.csv WHERE data_category!="N/A"
| fields system_name
| lookup itsi_service_map.csv system_name OUTPUT itsi_service
| join itsi_service [
    search index=itsi_summary earliest=-24h
    | stats latest(health_score) as health_score latest(severity) as severity by itsi_service
]
| eval breach_slo=if(health_score < 70 OR match(lower(severity),"(?i)critical|high"), 1, 0)
| where breach_slo=1
| table system_name, itsi_service, health_score, severity
```

## Visualization

Single value (services breaching SLO), Table (affected personal-data services), Line chart (health score trend), Heatmap (severity by hour).
